title: The effect of theta tACS on working memory
description: This dataset investigates the effects of theta transcranial alternating current stimulation (tACS) on working memory performance and neural activity. Participants received either verum or sham stimulation at 6 Hz over the Fpz-CPz electrode pair, followed by working memory tasks requiring mental manipulation or retention of items while EEG was recorded. Resting-state EEG recordings with eyes closed and open were also acquired to characterize baseline neural oscillations.
methods_description: Transcranial alternating current stimulation (tACS) was applied at 6 Hz, 1 mA intensity over Fpz-CPz for 20 minutes (verum or sham). Working memory tasks were performed during EEG recording. Resting-state EEG was recorded for 3 minutes with eyes closed and 1.5 minutes with eyes open.
